root.description: Region where the resource(s) will be managed. Defaults to the Region set in the provider configuration
root.name: region

root.description: Configuration block to support secret replication
root.name: replica

root.description: List of IAM policy documents that are merged together into the exported document. In merging, statements with non-blank `sid`s will override statements with the same `sid`
root.name: override_policy_documents

root.description: Determines whether or not Terraform will ignore changes made externally to `secret_string` or `secret_binary`. Changing this value after creation is a destructive operation
root.name: ignore_secret_changes

root.description: A structure that defines the rotation configuration for this secret
root.name: rotation_rules

root.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
root.name: tags

root.description: Number of days that AWS Secrets Manager waits before it can delete the secret. This value can be `0` to force deletion without recovery or range from `7` to `30` days. The default value is `30`
root.name: recovery_window_in_days

root.description: A map of IAM policy [statements](https://registry.terraform.io/providers/hashicorp/aws/latest/docs/data-sources/iam_policy_document#statement) for custom permission usage
root.name: policy_statements

root.description: Specifies text data that you want to encrypt and store in this version of the secret. This is required if `secret_binary` or `secret_string` is not set
root.name: secret_string_wo

root.description: Determines whether an ephemeral random password will be generated for `secret_string_wo`
root.name: create_random_password

root.description: The length of the generated random password
root.name: random_password_length

root.description: Specifies whether to rotate the secret immediately or wait until the next scheduled rotation window. The rotation schedule is defined in `rotation_rules`
root.name: rotate_immediately

root.description: Specifies the ARN of the Lambda function that can rotate the secret
root.name: rotation_lambda_arn

root.description: A description of the secret
root.name: description

root.description: Accepts boolean value to specify whether to overwrite a secret with the same name in the destination Region
root.name: force_overwrite_replica_secret

root.description: ARN or Id of the AWS KMS key to be used to encrypt the secret values in the versions stored in this secret. If you need to reference a CMK in a different account, you can use only the key ARN. If you don't specify this value, then Secrets Manager defaults to using the AWS account's default KMS key (the one named `aws/secretsmanager`
root.name: kms_key_id

root.description: Friendly name of the new secret. The secret name can consist of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, digits, and any of the following characters: `/_+=.@-`
root.name: name

root.description: Determines whether a policy will be created
root.name: create_policy

root.description: Specifies binary data that you want to encrypt and store in this version of the secret. This is required if `secret_string` or `secret_string_wo` is not set. Needs to be encoded to base64
root.name: secret_binary

root.description: Specifies text data that you want to encrypt and store in this version of the secret. This is required if `secret_binary` or `secret_string_wo` is not set
root.name: secret_string

root.description: Used together with `secret_string_wo` to trigger an update. Increment this value when an update to `secret_string_wo` is required
root.name: secret_string_wo_version

root.description: Determines whether resources will be created (affects all resources)
root.name: create

root.description: Creates a unique name beginning with the specified prefix
root.name: name_prefix

root.description: List of IAM policy documents that are merged together into the exported document. Statements must have unique `sid`s
root.name: source_policy_documents

root.description: Makes an optional API call to Zelkova to validate the Resource Policy to prevent broad access to your secret
root.name: block_public_policy

root.description: Specifies a list of staging labels that are attached to this version of the secret. A staging label must be unique to a single version of the secret
root.name: version_stages

root.description: Supply your own list of special characters to use for string generation. This overrides the default character list in the special argument
root.name: random_password_override_special

root.description: Determines whether secret rotation is enabled
root.name: enable_rotation

root.description: The name of the secret
root.name: secret_name

root.description: Attributes of the replica created
root.name: secret_replica

root.description: The secret string
root.name: secret_string

root.description: The secret binary
root.name: secret_binary

root.description: The unique identifier of the version of the secret
root.name: secret_version_id

root.description: The ARN of the secret
root.name: secret_arn

root.description: The ID of the secret
root.name: secret_id
